title: lovable.dev review: Build full-stack apps with AI (No-code revolution)
author: Ankit
text:
Member-only story
lovable.dev review: Build full-stack apps with AI (No-code revolution)
Are no-code AI tools finally good enough to replace coders — or just expensive hype in a prettier package?
What Exactly Is Lovable.dev?
Lovable.dev is an AI-powered platform that turns plain English prompts into full-stack web applications. We’re talking React + Tailwind CSS on the front end, Supabase handling your database, authentication, and storage on the back end — all generated from a text description.
You type something like: “Build a task management app with user login, real-time updates, and email notifications” — and lovable.dev builds it. Not a mockup. Not a wireframe. An actual deployable app.
It hit £13.5 million ARR just three months after launch in late 2024, with a growth rate of 2,816% that year. Co-founder Osaka has said there are at least 25,000 apps being built on the platform every single day. That’s not a niche tool — that’s a movement.
What Lovable.dev Is Actually Good At
Let me be real with you: for a specific use case, lovable.dev is genuinely impressive.
Speed to MVP is unreal. One user on Trustpilot said they did “6 months of work in 2 days.” A developer named Fahim ul Haq built an e-commerce prototype in 20 minutes — complete with Stripe and MongoDB integration. One Product Hunt reviewer said building their entire front-end UI/UX took “weeks instead of months.” These…
